description:
To properly set the permissions of /etc/sudoers.d, run the command: 
$ sudo chmod 0750 /etc/sudoers.d
rationale:
Setting correct permissions on the /etc/sudoers.d directory is important
because this directory hosts sudo configuration. Protection of this
directory is critical for system security. Restricting the permissions
ensures exclusive control of the sudo configuration.
ocil:
To check the permissions of /etc/sudoers.d, run the command: 
$ ls -l /etc/sudoers.d
If properly configured, the output should indicate the following permissions: 0750
fixtext:
Configure the "/etc/sudoers.d" file to "0750" by running the following command:
$ sudo chmod 0750 /etc/sudoers.d
srg_requirement: The Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 /etc/sudoers.d file must have mode 0750 or less permissive to prevent unauthorized access.
title: Verify Permissions On /etc/sudoers.d Directory
